key_findings: A_cooccur formula P(j|i)/P(i|j) is bounded by f_j/f_i < 1/3 when f_i > 3*f_j (pass criterion was misspecified)
56/71 letter features show A_cooccur > 0.25 (meaningful directional co-occurrence)
46/71 letter features show A_cooccur > 0.30 (strong directional asymmetry)
O_jaccard values computed for all 71 letter features; max=0.333 for absorbed features 2406, 3943
21726/24576 SAE latents active in 10,000 token corpus
Data format valid; arrays ready for B2 ARS_v2 computation
tasks.note: Co-occurrence matrix (24576 x 71) computed. Arrays saved to full/ and pilots/ for B2.

key_findings: encoder_norm remains best single detector: AUROC=0.7566 (95% CI: 0.661-0.851)
O_jaccard (Jaccard overlap with parent features) surprisingly strong: AUROC=0.7211, AUPRC=0.0748 (highest AUPRC!)
ARS_v2 (encoder_norm * A_cooccur) AUROC=0.5856 — significantly WORSE than encoder_norm alone (DeLong z=-2.455, p=0.993)
A_cooccur values bounded at 0.333 by mathematical construction (f_j < f_i/3 means ratio < 1/3) — no discriminative signal
ARS_original and ARS_full both AUROC~0.528 — co-occurrence combination degrades encoder_norm signal
O_jaccard has highest AUPRC (0.0748 vs 0.004 for encoder_norm) due to coverage of only 71 letter features
Primary hypothesis H_ARS: FAILED — ARS_v2 does NOT improve over encoder_norm alone
Key insight: A_cooccur formula is mathematically bounded by token frequency ratio, providing near-zero discrimination
tasks.note: H_ARS FAILED: ARS_v2 significantly degrades encoder_norm. A_cooccur bounded at 0.33 by construction. O_jaccard alone second strongest (AUROC=0.721) but only covers letter feature subset. Recommend: keep encoder_norm as primary detector; investigate O_jaccard as complementary signal in full experiment.
